title: batrachium paucistamineum subsp. trichophyllum
intro: Batrachium paucistamineum subsp. trichophyllum, also known as the lesser bladderwort, is a carnivorous plant species belonging to the family Lentibulariaceae. It has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antibacterial effects. The health benefits of this plant are attributed to its ability to reduce fever, alleviate respiratory issues, and even help manage diabetes. The herbal preparations of Batrachium paucistamineum subsp. trichophyllum typically involve infusions or decoctions of the plant's leaves or stems, which can be consumed as a tea or used topically for wound care.
